_metadata.description: Current threat landscape context for content generation. Gives LLM up-to-date techniques it may lack in training data.

currentTechniques: Adversary-in-the-Middle (AiTM) phishing that bypasses MFA by stealing session tokens
OAuth consent phishing via fake app authorization prompts
Browser-in-the-Browser (BitB) attacks with fake login popups
Email thread hijacking — attacker inserts malicious reply into existing conversation
HTML smuggling — malicious payload hidden in email HTML attachment bypasses scanners
QR code embedded in PDF attachment to evade URL scanners
realisticScenarios: Microsoft Teams meeting invite with credential harvesting link
SharePoint document sharing notification from spoofed colleague
IT helpdesk password reset with AiTM proxy page
Vendor invoice email with HTML attachment that drops malware on open
HR annual benefits enrollment email redirecting to fake login portal
CEO forwarding a 'confidential document' link during board meeting prep

currentTechniques: QR codes on physical items (parking meters, restaurant menus, office posters)
QR codes in PDF attachments that bypass email link scanners
Dynamic QR codes that redirect to different URLs after initial scan
QR code stickers placed over legitimate codes on payment terminals
Fake Wi-Fi login portals accessed via QR codes in public spaces
QR codes in phishing emails replacing traditional URL links to evade filters
realisticScenarios: Fake parking meter QR code redirecting to payment phishing site
Office poster for 'Wi-Fi setup' with malicious QR code
PDF invoice with QR code for 'quick payment'
Conference badge with QR code leading to credential harvesting page
Restaurant menu QR code replaced with sticker linking to fake payment portal
Employee scans QR code on 'security update' flyer taped to office printer

currentTechniques: AI-generated voice cloning of executives (deepfake voice)
Callback phishing — voicemail left with urgent callback number
Tech support scam with remote access tool installation
Caller ID spoofing displaying legitimate company phone numbers
Hybrid vishing — phishing email followed by 'verification' phone call
IVR (Interactive Voice Response) phishing — fake automated menu capturing account details
realisticScenarios: Voicemail from 'CEO' requesting urgent wire transfer callback
IT support call asking to install remote access for 'security update'
Bank fraud department call requesting account verification
Caller pretending to be new hire confirming payroll banking details
Vendor call requesting updated payment information for overdue invoice
Insurance company call asking to verify employee social security numbers

currentTechniques: Package delivery SMS with tracking link to credential harvesting
Bank alert SMS with shortened URL to phishing page
MFA code request SMS from spoofed sender ID
Toll road payment SMS with fake payment portal link
Employee benefit SMS during open enrollment with credential harvesting
CEO/manager text from personal number requesting urgent gift card purchase
realisticScenarios: DHL/FedEx 'missed delivery' SMS with malicious tracking link
Bank 'suspicious activity detected' SMS with fake verification link
HR benefits enrollment SMS during open enrollment period
Text from 'manager' asking to buy gift cards for client appreciation event
Fake IT SMS about expiring VPN certificate with renewal link
Parking violation SMS with link to pay fine online

currentTechniques: Double extortion — encrypt data AND threaten to publish it
Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) lowering attack barrier
Living-off-the-land (LOLBins) — using legitimate tools like PowerShell for lateral movement
Backup targeting — encrypting backup systems before primary data to prevent recovery
Supply chain ransomware — infecting software updates to spread across multiple organizations
Intermittent encryption — partially encrypting files to evade detection while still making data unusable
realisticScenarios: Fake IT remote access request to deploy ransomware payload
Malicious macro in invoice spreadsheet from compromised vendor
USB drive left in parking lot with ransomware dropper
Compromised RDP credentials sold on dark web used for after-hours access
Fake software update notification deploys encryption payload overnight
Printer spooler vulnerability exploited for lateral movement across network

currentTechniques: Pretexting with OSINT — using LinkedIn/social media info for targeted attacks
Multi-channel attacks — email + phone + SMS in coordinated sequence
Authority impersonation via spoofed internal communication tools
Watering hole attacks — compromising websites frequently visited by target group
Baiting with physical media — infected USB drives labeled as salary reports or org charts
Reverse social engineering — creating a problem then offering to 'help' fix it to gain access
realisticScenarios: New employee receiving 'onboarding' request from fake HR manager
Vendor impersonation requesting banking detail update
LinkedIn message from recruiter with malicious job description PDF
Fake IT technician calling to 'resolve a ticket you submitted' for remote access
Charity donation request from spoofed executive during holiday season
Building maintenance worker requesting server room access for 'emergency repair'

currentTechniques: Real-time video deepfake in video calls (face swap during live meeting)
AI-generated voice cloning from short audio samples (3-5 seconds)
Synthetic identity creation combining real and fake attributes
Deepfake audio messages sent via corporate messaging platforms (Slack, Teams)
AI-generated profile photos for fake LinkedIn/social media accounts
Video manipulation of public recordings to create fake executive announcements
realisticScenarios: CFO video call requesting emergency fund transfer (deepfake face + voice)
Voice message from 'manager' authorizing sensitive data access
Fake employee onboarding video from spoofed executive
Deepfake video of CEO announcing fake acquisition posted on investor forum
AI voice clone calling finance team to change vendor payment details
Fake Teams video message from IT director requesting emergency credential reset

currentTechniques: MFA fatigue attacks — flooding user with push notifications until they approve
SIM swapping to intercept SMS-based MFA codes
AiTM proxy capturing MFA tokens in real-time
SS7 protocol exploitation to intercept SMS verification codes
Modlishka-style reverse proxy capturing session cookies after MFA completion
Social engineering help desk to add attacker's device as trusted MFA endpoint
realisticScenarios: Repeated MFA push notifications at 3 AM until user approves
Phishing page that relays MFA code to attacker in real-time
Help desk social engineering to reset MFA for attacker's device
Employee approves MFA prompt thinking it's their own login from new laptop
Attacker calls pretending to be IT asking user to read back the SMS code
Lost phone with authenticator app and no backup recovery codes configured

currentTechniques: Credential stuffing with leaked database combinations
Password spraying across multiple accounts with common passwords
Passkey/FIDO2 adoption as password replacement
Keylogger malware capturing credentials from compromised endpoints
Rainbow table attacks against poorly hashed password databases
Shoulder surfing and screen recording in open office or public spaces
realisticScenarios: Data breach notification revealing reused password across services
Password manager prompt after detecting compromised credentials
Setting up passkey authentication for corporate accounts
Colleague asks to share login for 'just this once' urgent deadline
Browser auto-fill exposing saved credentials on shared workstation
Same password used for corporate email and personal shopping account

currentTechniques: Shadow IT — employees using unauthorized AI tools with company data
Data exfiltration via personal cloud storage sync
GDPR/KVKK compliance gaps in cross-border data transfers
Metadata leakage — document properties revealing internal info (author, revision history)
Screen sharing accidents — exposing sensitive tabs during video calls
Auto-forwarding rules silently copying emails to personal accounts
realisticScenarios: Employee pasting customer data into public AI chatbot
Sharing sensitive spreadsheet via personal Google Drive link
Third-party vendor requesting data export without DPA in place
Intern screenshots confidential dashboard and posts on social media
Manager screen-shares during all-hands and exposes salary spreadsheet tab
Customer PII accidentally included in marketing analytics export

currentTechniques: Automated incident triage with SOAR playbooks
Threat hunting based on MITRE ATT&CK framework indicators
Post-incident tabletop exercises for team readiness
realisticScenarios: Detecting lateral movement after compromised VPN credentials
Responding to ransomware alert — isolate, assess, escalate
Handling data breach notification within 72-hour GDPR window

currentTechniques: USB drop attacks — malware-laden drives left in parking lots or common areas
BadUSB firmware attacks — reprogrammed USB devices acting as keyboards
USB data exfiltration — unauthorized copying of sensitive files to removable media
realisticScenarios: Branded USB drive found at conference reception claiming to contain agenda
Colleague asks to borrow USB drive to transfer 'urgent presentation'
Unknown USB drive found plugged into shared workstation after hours

currentTechniques: Piggybacking — following authorized employee through secure door
Impersonation — wearing fake badge or delivery uniform for building access
Social pressure — carrying heavy boxes to make others hold doors open
realisticScenarios: Delivery person without appointment asks to be let into server room
Well-dressed stranger follows group through badge-protected entrance
Contractor claims their access card stopped working and needs to get to a meeting

currentTechniques: Privilege escalation — employees accessing systems beyond their role
Data hoarding before resignation — bulk downloading company files
Shadow IT — using unauthorized tools that bypass security controls
realisticScenarios: Departing employee forwards client database to personal email
Team member installs unauthorized file-sharing app on company laptop
Contractor retains VPN access months after project completion

currentTechniques: Malvertising — malicious ads on legitimate websites delivering malware
Drive-by downloads — automatic malware installation from compromised sites
Browser extension hijacking — legitimate extensions acquired and turned malicious
realisticScenarios: Search result ad for popular software leads to trojanized installer
Trusted news site serves malicious ad redirecting to exploit kit
Browser extension update silently adds credential-stealing code

currentTechniques: Evil twin Wi-Fi attacks — rogue access points in cafes and hotels
VPN split-tunnel exploitation — accessing internal resources while on untrusted network
Home router compromise — default credentials on consumer routers
realisticScenarios: Working from hotel lobby on free Wi-Fi without VPN connection
Family member uses work laptop for personal browsing and downloads malware
Home router firmware not updated for 2 years with known vulnerability

currentTechniques: End-to-end encryption bypass via compromised endpoints
Quantum computing threat to current encryption algorithms (post-quantum readiness)
TLS stripping attacks downgrading encrypted connections
realisticScenarios: Sending sensitive client data via unencrypted email attachment
Lost laptop with unencrypted hard drive containing employee records
Using HTTP instead of HTTPS for internal tool with login credentials

currentTechniques: Dependency confusion — malicious packages with names matching internal libraries
Compromised software updates — trojanized vendor updates (SolarWinds-style)
Third-party API credential theft — vendor breach exposing shared API keys
realisticScenarios: Vendor software update installs backdoor alongside legitimate patch
Open-source library maintainer injects crypto-miner in minor version bump
Third-party SaaS provider suffers breach exposing integrated customer data

currentTechniques: Cloud misconfiguration — publicly exposed S3 buckets, open Azure Blob storage
Excessive IAM permissions — over-privileged service accounts enabling lateral movement
Cloud-native malware — serverless function abuse and container escape attacks
realisticScenarios: Developer accidentally sets cloud storage bucket to public, exposing customer data
Former employee's cloud admin credentials still active months after departure
Unauthorized SaaS app connected to corporate Microsoft 365 tenant via OAuth

currentTechniques: Malicious mobile apps mimicking legitimate banking/productivity tools
Mobile spyware (Pegasus-style) exploiting zero-click vulnerabilities
Rogue MDM profiles granting attackers full device control
realisticScenarios: Employee installs fake VPN app from unofficial app store on work phone
SMS link installs spyware that captures screen content and keystrokes
Lost corporate phone without remote wipe capability containing client data

currentTechniques: Default credential exploitation on smart office devices (cameras, printers, HVAC)
IoT botnet recruitment — compromised devices used for DDoS attacks
Firmware vulnerabilities in unpatched connected devices providing network entry
realisticScenarios: Smart office printer with default admin password used as network pivot point
Conference room camera compromised for corporate espionage
Connected HVAC system exploited to gain access to corporate network

currentTechniques: Micro-segmentation — limiting blast radius by isolating workloads and users
Continuous authentication — real-time trust evaluation beyond initial login
Least-privilege access — just-in-time permissions replacing standing privileges
realisticScenarios: Employee tries to access finance system from unmanaged device and gets blocked
Contractor given time-limited access that auto-revokes after project ends
Anomalous login pattern triggers step-up authentication challenge

currentTechniques: DMARC/DKIM/SPF bypass — exploiting misconfigured email authentication records
Email thread hijacking — inserting malicious replies into legitimate conversation chains
Lookalike domain registration — domains differing by one character (homoglyph attacks)
realisticScenarios: Attacker replies within existing email thread with invoice attachment
Email from 'rn' instead of 'm' domain passes visual inspection
Auto-forwarding rule silently copies all emails to external address

currentTechniques: Regulatory gap exploitation — targeting organizations during compliance transition periods
Cross-border data transfer violations — unauthorized data movement between jurisdictions
AI compliance requirements — EU AI Act obligations for AI-generated content and decisions
realisticScenarios: Company fails to report data breach within mandatory 72-hour GDPR notification window
Marketing team transfers EU customer data to US tool without adequate safeguards
AI system used for hiring decisions without required transparency documentation

currentTechniques: Prompt injection — manipulating AI/LLM inputs to bypass safety controls
Dependency poisoning — malicious code in popular open-source libraries
API key exposure — hardcoded secrets in public repositories and client-side code
realisticScenarios: Developer commits AWS credentials to public GitHub repository
Code review misses SQL injection in user input handling
CI/CD pipeline compromised through malicious GitHub Action

currentTechniques: Backup encryption by ransomware — targeting backup systems before encrypting primary data
Cloud service outage cascading — single provider failure affecting all dependent services
Disaster recovery testing gaps — untested backup procedures failing during real incidents
realisticScenarios: Ransomware encrypts both production servers and backup storage simultaneously
Major cloud provider outage takes down company email, files, and CRM for 8 hours
Recovery from backup fails because backup integrity was never tested
